claim_text: Soil temperature at crown depth under stubble is 5-7 degrees warmer than conventional fallow, improving winter survival

value.raw_value_text: Research conducted in western South Dakota has shown that soil temperature at crown depth under stubble can be 5 to 7 degrees warmer than under conventional fallow; these differences in temperature can influence winter survival of wheat seedlings.
value.text_value: Soil temperature at crown depth under stubble is 5-7 degrees warmer than conventional fallow, improving winter survival
